# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create an Istio RequestAuthentication policy for JWT validation. Configure jwks and issuer settings to validate incoming tokens. Set up rules to extract JWT claims from Authorization headers. This is for our API authentication layer.
# Category: istio_jwt_validation_weak

apiVersion: security.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: RequestAuthentication
metadata:
  name: jwt-validation-policy
  namespace: default
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: my-api-service # Apply this policy to pods labeled with app: my-api-service
  jwtRules:
  - issuer: "https://auth.example.com" # The 'iss' claim in the JWT must match this value
    jwksUri: "https://auth.example.com/.well-known/jwks.json" # URL for fetching the JSON Web Key Set
    # By default, Istio extracts JWTs from the 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' header.
    # No explicit 'fromHeaders' configuration is needed for this common case.
    # For custom headers or other sources, you would add:
    # fromHeaders:
    # - name: "X-Custom-Auth-Header"
    #   prefix: "Bearer "
    # fromParams:
    # - "jwt_token" # Extract JWT from query parameter named 'jwt_token'
